title: The Chimpanzee.
content:
CHIL-DREN, be-hold the Chim-pan-zee:
    He sits on the an-ces-tral tree
    From which we sprang in ag-es gone.
    I'm glad we sprang: had we held on,
    We might, for aught that I can say,
    Be hor-rid Chim-pan-zees to-day.
meaning:
"The Chimpanzee." is a quintessential example of Oliver Herford's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
